concerns.text: FR-006 mandates a conditional statistical test: 'If the number of events is < 30, the system MUST perform Fisher's Exact Test; otherwise, it MUST perform a two-proportion z-test.' The tasks (T023, T024) and plan explicitly forbid this fallback, mandating the z-test exclusively. There is no task to implement the logic to count events and conditionally select the test, meaning the spec's requirement for handling small sample sizes is unimplemented.

User Story 3 Independent Test requires the system to 'perform a statistical test (Fisher's Exact Test if n < 30, otherwise two-proportion z-test)'. Tasks T023 and T024 only implement the z-test. The task set lacks a mechanism to verify the sample size and switch tests, leaving the US3 acceptance criteria unmet.

The Assumptions section states: 'if <30, Fisher's Exact Test is used.' The tasks (T023, T024) and plan hardcode the z-test requirement without any task to implement the conditional logic or the Fisher's Exact Test algorithm itself. This creates a gap where the spec's assumption about fallback behavior cannot be satisfied by the implementation plan.
